title: GraPhlAn Visualization of Single and Multiple MetaPhlAn Samples
author: Curtis Huttenhower
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d9s96d

steps.title: Generate GraPhlAn Input Files
steps.instruction: The "metaphlan2graphlan.py" script in the plotting_scripts folder can generate the two required input files for GraPhlAn: (i) a tree structure and (ii) graphical annotation options. Create a temporary directory and generate tree and annotation files from a profiled sample using the metaphlan2graphlan.py script. $ mkdir tmp $ plotting_scripts/metaphlan2graphlan.py profiled_samples/BM_SRS013506.txt --tree_file tmp/BM_SRS013506.tree.txt --annot_file tmp/BM_SRS013506.annot.txt

steps.title: Run GraPhlAn and Generate Image
steps.instruction: Run GraPhlAn using the generated tree and annotation files to create an XML representation, then convert it to a PNG image. Refer to the GraPhlAn project page for more details and additional customization options. $ graphlan_annotate.py --annot tmp/BM_SRS013506.annot.txt tmp/BM_SRS013506.tree.txt tmp/BM_SRS013506.xml $ graphlan.py --dpi 200 tmp/BM_SRS013506.xml output_images/BM_SRS013506.png

steps.title: View Resulting Image
steps.instruction: The previous step will generate a GraPhlAn image.

steps.title: Process Multiple Samples with Customization
steps.instruction: To process multiple samples, use a loop script similar to this example. The metaphlan2graphlan.py script offers customization options like --max_annot_clades (default 10), --min_annot_lev, and --max_annot_lev to control the visualization. $ mkdir -p tmp $ for file in profiled_samples/* $ do $ filename=`basename ${file}` $ samplename=${filename%.*} $ plotting_scripts/metaphlan2graphlan.py ${file} --tree_file tmp/${samplename}.tree.txt --annot_file tmp/${samplename}.annot.txt $ graphlan_annotate.py --annot tmp/${samplename}.annot.txt tmp/${samplename}.tree.txt tmp/${samplename}.xml $ graphlan.py --dpi 200 tmp/${samplename}.xml output_images/${samplename}.png $ done

steps.title: View Customization Options
steps.instruction: To view all available customization options for metaphlan2graphlan.py, execute the help command. $ plotting_scripts/metaphlan2graphlan.py -h

steps.title: Manually Customize Annotation File
steps.instruction: For advanced customization, the annotation file can be manually edited or modified using ad-hoc scripts. Refer to the GraPhlAn readme and examples for additional information.

steps.title: Visualize Merged Abundance Data
steps.instruction: The GraPhlAn visualization workflow can also be applied to a merged relative abundance table. In this case, the abundances reported refer to the overall average of each clade across all the samples in the table. $ plotting_scripts/metaphlan2graphlan.py output/merged_abundance_table.txt --tree_file tmp/merged.tree.txt --annot_file tmp/merged.annot.txt $ graphlan_annotate.py --annot tmp/merged.annot.txt tmp/merged.tree.txt tmp/merged.xml $ graphlan.py --dpi 200 tmp/merged.xml output_images/merged.png

steps.title: Generate Class Comparisons
steps.instruction: Manual and script-based modification of the annotation file can facilitate comparisons between different classes in the GraPhlAn visualization.

steps.title: Export MetaPhlAn Output to Krona
steps.instruction: MetaPhlAn output can also be exported for visualization with Krona (another popular visualization tool) using the "metaphlan2krona.py" script, located in the "conversion_scripts" folder downloaded as part of the MetaPhlAn package.
